summary: Implemented conversation action tagging system with inference, confirmation, and manual tagging modes. Created action_tagger.py module with 8 core functions for detecting action conversations, tagging them to tasks, and managing their lifecycle. Successfully tested all functionality including inference accuracy, tagging/retrieval, retagging, and closing. Database schema includes proper indexes and constraints.
notes_for_orchestrator: D1.2 is complete and ready for integration with D2.1 (thread-close hooks). Key integration points: (1) On conversation start, call infer_action_conversation() and prompt for confirmation; (2) After confirmation, call tag_conversation(); (3) On conversation close, D2.1 should call get_task_for_conversation() to get task_id, then trigger task status update. The close_action_conversation() function is available for D2.1 to call after task status is updated. Note: Task IDs are stored as TEXT in action_conversations.db even though they're INTEGER in tasks.db - the module handles conversion automatically. Cross-database JOINs don't work in SQLite, so get_conversation_details() queries separately and merges in Python.
lessons_learned: Task IDs in tasks.db are INTEGER, not TEXT. Must convert to string when storing in action_conversations.db and querying.
Cannot JOIN across SQLite databases. When querying tasks.db from action_tagger.py, query separately and merge results in Python.
Action inference patterns need better capture groups to extract task names from phrases like 'memo for X'. The second capture group (for X) is what matters.
